title: Extract UTF-8 cursor movement helpers to prevent divergence
description:
UTF-8 boundary-aware cursor movement code appears in three places with similar but slightly different logic:

1. Left arrow (lines 759-766): move_cursor_left()
2. Right arrow (lines 768-776): move_cursor_right()
3. Backspace (lines 744-752): delete_char_before_cursor()

Each has:
    let mut pos = state.input_cursor - 1;  // or +1
    while !state.input.is_char_boundary(pos) {
        pos -= 1;  // or += 1
    }

Problem: If a UTF-8 boundary bug is found, it might be fixed in one place but not others. Different edge case handling:
- Left goes backward from cursor-1
- Right goes forward from cursor+1 (with bounds check)
- Backspace has no bounds check

Extract:
    fn cursor_left(s: &str, cursor: usize) -> usize { ... }
    fn cursor_right(s: &str, cursor: usize) -> usize { ... }

These will:
- Ensure consistent UTF-8 safety across all movement
- Make edge cases testable in isolation
- Prevent divergence
close_reason: deferred to backlog; P3/P4 style issues, not blocking epic completion
